original_text:
For self-support students and holders of externally-managed scholarships
(externally-supported/sponsored students who have their own funds):
(1) With regard to self-paying students and students with external sponsors,
first semester/term fees must be paid in advance and/or fully paid upon
enrolment, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.

(2) Continuing full-time postgraduate (PG) students (Master, Doctor and
Diploma), i.e., those who completed at least one semester/term, will be
invoiced for the applicable registration, tuition, and other fees each
semester/term from the second semester/term onwards, which must be
paid in advance and/or fully paid before each semester/term starts,
otherwise they will not be allowed to register.

The invoice must be paid in advance and/or fully
paid up before each semester/term starts, otherwise they will not be
allowed to register.

The invoice must be paid
in advance and/or fully paid up before each semester/term starts,
otherwise they will not be allowed to register.

(5) Continuing part-time, including flexible program, students will be
invoiced for the applicable registration, tuition, and other fees each
semester/term from the second semester/term onwards, which must be
paid in advance and/or fully paid before each semester/term starts,
otherwise they will not be allowed to register.

(7) If tuition and other fees remain unpaid by the start of a semester/term, a
student’s status shall be ‘suspended due to financial inability’.

Thus, they cannot
enroll in any course, nor can they avail of student privileges and benefits
during the suspended period such as use of an AIT ID card, access to
SIS and registration records, staying in AIT accommodation, use of
Library and School facilities, discount of children in the AIT
International School (AITIS), etc.

Their educational visas may also be
cancelled.

(10) Additional payments for audited and repeated courses, as well as beyond
the minimum 3 credits for part-time students, registered for the
semester/term must be fully paid one week before the mid-
semester/term examination period starts.

FB-6-1-1: CREDIT POLICY
(12) If fees remain unpaid after the deadline for adding/dropping of courses
of the semester/term in which a student was ‘suspended due to financial
inability’, the student shall be recommended for dismissal.

Once dismissed,
students who are staying in campus accommodation shall be asked to
vacate their dormitory rooms; and their educational visas will be
cancelled.

Their student status can only be reinstated
upon full payment of the applicable registration, tuition and other fees
for the following semester/term, which must be paid in advance and/or
fully paid before the semester/term starts.

(16) The student cannot avail of the facility of non-payment one more time
during the reinstated or re-admitted period.

Permanent dismissal will be
applied to those who will be dismissed again due to non-payment of fees
on time.

(20) Overdue accounts shall be reviewed periodically, and follow-up
collections shall be initiated.

Sponsors must be invoiced for outstanding
dues and requested to send AIT a promissory note detailing when and
how payment will be made.

(6) The Office of Student Affairs (OSA) shall regularly follow up with
external donor partners to ensure that payments are disbursed.

Writing-off of doubtful accounts will be requested by OFIN and have to be
approved by the President, or their VP designate.

Student Accommodation Rental
(1) Students are required to pay a security deposit, which is collected in
advance.

Otherwise, concerned students have to vacate
the room within two weeks after the given deadline to pay.

(6) If accommodation and other fees remain unpaid beyond two months, a
student’s status shall be ‘suspended/dismissed due to financial inability’.

Employee Accommodation Rental
(1) Employees are required to pay a two-month deposit, which is collected
in advance.

(4) If a tenant defaults on any payment for a period of two successive
months, the housing lease agreement may be terminated by written
Page 5/9

FB-6-1-1: CREDIT POLICY
notice from the Accommodation Unit, and the tenant is required to
vacate the housing unit within two weeks.

Writing-off of outstanding invoices, including interest due, will be requested by
the OFAM Director and have to be approved by the President, or their VP
designate, and forwarded to OFIN for action.

All Requests for Travel Authorization (RTA) and Promissory Note (PN) by
employees are required to be settled by the indicated due dates.

With regard to parents (AIT students and employees) with more than one child:
(1) Fees for the first child shall be paid in full within 30 days from the first
day of the semester.

(1) Fees for the rest of the children shall be paid in full by the end of the
second month of the semester.

With regard to other students, fees shall be paid in full within 30 days from the
first day of the semester.

If the payment is not received within the periods stipulated, the AITIS students
concerned shall be removed from the rolls of the school and shall not be allowed
to attend classes.

Lessees are required to pay a three-month advance deposit to take on the lease
of premises.

Dining
(1) Personal meals are required to be settled.
No credit shall be granted.

(2) Meals that are charged to non-personal accounts must be authorized and
payment is to be settled through internal charge.
Invoices are required to be settled by the indicated due date.

Overdue accounts
shall be reviewed periodically and follow-up on collections shall be initiated.

FB-6-1-1: CREDIT POLICY
Approved by: President’s Executive Council – 24 May 2005

source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf
original_text:
Research may be
sponsored by a government agency, private or non-government organization,
foundation, or individual.

source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf
original_text:
Contracted research may
entail confidentiality and restriction on publication.

source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf
original_text:
Authors must be
responsible for what has been written in return for the credit and recognition that
authorship brings.

source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf
original_text:
For any multi-authored article based on a student thesis, research study or
dissertation, the student should normally be the first author, unless there is
documented evidence that the journal required that the authorship should be
written in a different order.

source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf
original_text:
However, the Chair of the student’s Program
Committee should serve as the corresponding author with any journal.

source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf
original_text:
Use of data of a confidential nature, particularly that of a contracted research
where secrecy may be necessary for a limited period, for one’s personal advantage
or that of a third party.

source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf
original_text:
DISCIPLINARY ACTION
Any member of the AIT community who is aware of academic misconduct in
research or publication may report the incident or practice to the School Dean or
directly to the Vice President for Academic Affairs.

source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf
original_text:
The procedures for disciplinary actions shall be according to the Institute Policy and
Procedures on Employment of Faculty and of Staff: General Work Regulations and
Student Handbook (section on Student Code of Conduct).

source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf
original_text:
• (19 Nov 2010) For a multi-authored article, the Chair of the student’s Program
Committee should serve as the corresponding author with any journal;
Transferred management of the electronic plagiarism checking to the IT
Services Unit (from the Library).

Students who misplace their accommodation unit keys may ask the

If this service is required after office

Students who cook in dormitories where cooking is prohibited may face a

Students disturbing the peace of their surroundings shall receive a formal
reprimand as a first warning.

Sub-letting rooms may result
in eviction from student accommodation.

Students who do not upkeep the standard conditions of the common area and

Special and exchange students are required to pay the rent in full for the whole

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
To
ensure fairness, measures may be taken to compensate for historical and
social disadvantages that prevent women and men from operating on a
level field.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The accomplishments, activities and individual differences of other
people must be respected.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
Members of the Institute’s community must not abuse the services,
benefits, opportunities and facilities of the Institute, nor use them for
their personal financial gain.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
AIT employees, students and residents must act in the interests of the
Institute and avoid engaging in any actions contrary to the policies and
interests of the Institute.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
AIT employees in supervisory positions must at all times use their
authority ethically, with respect and sensitivity, and in accordance with
the Institute’s policies.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
An employee must disclose to their supervisor any actual or potential
conflict of interest with the interests of the Institute, which may arise
from outside activities, professional or otherwise.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
When expressing personal opinions outside the Institute, AIT employees
and students are required to note explicitly that the opinions expressed
are personal and do not necessarily represent the position of the Institute.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
Acceptance by an employee of a gift valued at THB 3,000 or more must
be reported by the employee to their Unit Head within 15 days of
acceptance, giving details as to the description of the item, sources and
circumstance leading to the acceptance.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text: Items valued at less than THB 3,000 may be accepted without reporting.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
No employee or student shall deny or grant a right or a privilege to
another employee or student wholly or partly on the basis of nationality,
race, social background, gender, age, marital status, family status, sexual
orientation, or disability.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The Institute is responsible for finding an appropriate solution for the
grievances of a member of the community and for providing protection
to that person and all persons concerned from any undesirable
consequence that may result from their grievances.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text: A Grievance Committee shall receive all grievances.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The Grievance Committee shall
elect a Chair from among its members, which will have a term of two
years and can serve for not more than two consecutive terms.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The Grievance Committee shall perform three main roles:
a.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The Grievance Committee must be prepared to identify
breaches of the Institute P&Ps, etc.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
In performing their duties, they
must protect the interests of all members of the AIT community.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The Grievance Committee should express in writing
what came to light during the processing of grievances.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
They must
inform the President of all settlements made during the grievance
process.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
A complainant is required to initiate the grievance process by
submitting the grievance in writing (please refer to the Grievance
/ Complaint Fact Sheet in the Annex as a guide) to the Grievance
Committee or discussing their grievance in person with any
member of the Grievance Committee.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The main exception to the provisions above is when it is
suspected that a serious crime has been committed, or may be
committed unless preventive measures are taken.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
When there
are strong grounds to conclude that a serious crime such as rape,
grievous bodily harm, assault, or theft has been committed, the
Grievance Committee should consult with the President, and if
necessary, inform the police.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
‘Direct confrontation’ involves the complainant writing a
statement to the individual(s) concerned, describing the
unwelcome behavior and its effect, and stating that the behavior
must cease.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The complainant may seek the advice of the Grievance
Committee on what to say or write.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The person(s) towards whom the direct confrontation is directed
may likewise use consultation and direct confrontation in their
response.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
A mediator may be proposed either by the Grievance Committee,
or by either one or more of the parties to the complaint, and
whose intervention as a mediator is acceptable to both sides.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
Outcomes of the mediation shall include explicit written
agreements to be submitted to the Grievance Committee.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
If the informal grievance process, fails to lead to a satisfactory
resolution of the complaint, the complainant who has suffered
from harassment, abuse or discrimination may lodge a formal
complaint with the Grievance Committee.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The formal complaint must be in writing, and should state clearly
all facts for consideration.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The grievance must include:
i.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The Grievance Committee is required to address the complaint
in utmost confidentiality and with due regard to the privacy of
the complainant, and of the person accused (‘the defendant’).

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
It is possible that a valid grievance may arise during
proceedings which would not necessarily be excluded from the
grievance procedure.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
When the Grievance Committee is
involved in a grievance procedure, they should keep in
mind that facts are the substance of any grievance, and the
fact-gathering process is of fundamental importance.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
In gathering the facts, the Grievance
Committee should put all points in writing even if the
grievance is not presented.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
In obtaining evidence, the
Grievance Committee should concentrate on recording
facts, not arguments.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
Having gathered the relevant
facts, the Grievance Committee should proceed to analyze
the grievance.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
When further action is required, the President shall appoint a
Grievance Tribunal.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The Grievance Tribunal shall be convened at the earliest possible
date after the formal complaint.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
Hearings shall be open only to the members of the
Grievance Tribunal, the complainant(s) and the defendant, and
any witnesses called.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The Grievance Tribunal must seek to ascertain, to the best of its
ability, the facts of the case.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
In ascertaining the facts of the case, the Tribunal may call
witnesses to testify before the Grievance Tribunal.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
Both the
complainant and the defendant may be called as witnesses.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
After having received the report of the Grievance Tribunal, the
President may take further legal advice, before acting.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The President may delegate their responsibilities to a Vice
President.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
When the defendant is an employee, pending the report of the
Grievance Tribunal, the President may, if they consider such
action justified, suspend the defendant from work until
completion of the investigation.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
A copy of the report shall be kept in the Office of the President, and be
accessible to the immediate Supervisor, Director of OHRS, Director of
OSA, or the Director of OAA.

source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf
original_text:
The Grievance Tribunal will recommend whether a copy of the report
should be placed in the defendant’s file in OHRS or OSA.

decision of the President, they may appeal to the Board, through its

However, changes in regulations
and procedures may be made before the next edition of the Student Handbook is

If
make-up classes are scheduled out of regular class hours, the re-scheduled
classes should not clash with the schedules of students in the class.

Students have the right to expect that their grade in a course should be
determined only by academic achievement consistent with the aims and content
of that course.

At the beginning of the course, the instructor should make known
the criteria that will be considered in determining the grade such as class
attendance, class participation, papers, examinations, projects and
presentations.

No one may be
excluded on the basis of nationality, race, social background, gender, age,
marital status, family status, sexual orientation, disability, economic status, or
political belief or affiliation.

Students must use the AIT Library and all educational resources of the Institute
in a responsible manner.

Students may not deface or vandalize books,
periodicals, and/or computer resources in the AIT Library as well as computer
lab hardware, software and related equipment.

Students must abide by the
Institute’s acceptable use policy for IT resources.

While students have the right to freedom of expression, including the right to
dissent, protest, or take reasoned exception to the information and views
offered in any course, this expression must not interfere with the rights of
others, impede the progress of instruction, or disrupt the processes of the
Institute.

Concerns about what constitutes misconduct either in general or specific situations
may be referred to the Director, OSA for general advice.

Academic Misconduct
Student Academic Misconduct means:
a) breach of such rules or guidelines relating to student academic conduct as
may be prescribed by the AIT Board and the Policy and Procedure
Statements defined by the AIT Administration and the Academic Senate;
b) misconduct relating to assessment or examinations; and
c) any other conduct (the general nature of which has been made known to
students) regarded as misconduct according to current academic usage.

If there is suspicion of cheating during an examination, in an
assignment or a research project, the instructor/invigilator should
immediately call the attention of the student and put a notation on segments
of the exam, assignment or project where cheating has been suspiciously
committed.

The instructor/invigilator should seek an explanation from
the student at the end of the examination or when the assignment or project
was submitted, and submit an incident report to the Department Head, who
should then notify the School Dean.

Notes of the interview will be recorded and
should be agreed upon by all parties.

If the interview panel is satisfied that there
is no evidence of cheating, the Department Head and the
instructor/invigilator may recommend to the School Dean to give no penalty.

However, if cheating was proven, the
Department Head and the instructor/invigilator may recommend to the
School Dean to disregard the original examination score and require the
student to submit an equivalent piece of work or retake a different version
of the examination.

Upon receipt of the retake, the School Dean may decide
to:
● Downgrade by 1 step the grade the student will receive upon repetition
of the exam or resubmission of the assignment/project;
● Give a score of ‘zero’ for the specific exam, project or assignment; or
● Give a failing grade or “F” for the course; or
● Recommend the student for suspension or dismissal.

The appeal should be
addressed to the School Dean.

If School Dean feels that there
is evidence of serious dishonesty and the recommendation of the Field of
Study Coordination and instructor/invigilator was suspension or dismissal,
the matter should be elevated to the Vice President for Academic Affairs.

● The investigating committee should consider all the available evidence,
including that of the incident report submitted by the School, and the
student accused.

This report should be submitted promptly, if
possible permitting the required action to be taken before the beginning
of the next semester.

The Vice President for Academic Affairs may:
● No penalty; or
● Downgrade by 1 step the grade the student will receive upon repetition
of the exam or resubmission of the assignment/project;
● Give a score of ‘zero’ for the specific exam, project or assignment; or
● Give a failing grade or “F” for the course; or
● Suspend the student from the Institute for a specific period; or
● Recommend dismissal especially if there is evidence that the incident is
already the second offence, a report to the Academic Senate should be
made for decision.

The appeal should be
addressed to the Vice President for Academic Affairs.

A student found guilty of grave cheating by the School
Dean, the Vice President for Academic Affairs and the Academic Senate may
be dismissed.

If the student pursues an appeal to the President, the Tribunal
of Appeals may be convened.

A complaint against a student should be made to the Director, OSA.

If the
matter cannot be settled informally the complaint must be confirmed in
writing and be presented by the complainant to the Director, OSA, who will
decide whether or not to proceed with an investigation.

If an investigation is authorized, a meeting of the Standing
Disciplinary Commission will be convened thru the Vice President for
Academic Affairs (who is also the Chair), which constitutes the
following membership:
● Secretary of the Commission (to be appointed by the VPAA)
● Director, Office of Student Affairs (OSA)
Additional members for cases involving students may consist of:
● Dean concerned
● Department Head concerned
● Student Advisor
● Coordinator, Student Welfare Unit
● Student Union President
b.

The student against whom the complaint has been made shall have
the right to be present while the Commission hears the evidence
against him or her.
He/She may question witnesses.

New Online Payment Options for Students
(as of 29 April 2021)
As part of Finance Department customer service enhancements and with the current COVID-19
situation, starting from 1st May 2021, students will be provided with several online payment options for
paying any invoice (Tuition & Fee, Accommodation, etc.).
In order to make use of this feature, students must login to SIS/SOMSIS, and click the menu Invoice.

From the invoice list shown, students should select the invoice they want to pay and click the button
"Pay Online".

Thai QR code:
Students must have an account at any Thai bank and its mobile app installed in their phone.

Credit card (Visa/Master/JCB)
Students must have a valid Visa/Master/JCB card.

WeChat/Alipay
Students must have a valid WeChat/Alipay wallet and its app must be installed on the phone.

AIT Finance Office is pleased to introduce these new payment methods to students and although
students may still make payments by directly transferring money to the AIT bank account and send

Medical Insurance/AIT Student Health Benefits Program
All new AIT degree program students (except those under EMBA-BKK and Special
Program students) are required to take compulsory standard medical insurance plan
from Generali Life Assurance (Thailand) Co.

Please be informed that due to the COVID-19 pandemic, you may check your member
status, claim status, benefits and channels to contact the Generali Insurance Call Center
via mobile application (E card).

If you have not yet filled up the Generali application form (in attachment), please
kindly complete it and return the form to Email : student-welfare@ait.ac.th
Any student who may want upgraded health benefits or enrol their dependents (spouse
and children) may avail of the other options provided by the company as described in
their manual.

Donor and externally-funded students may consult the Admissions and Scholarships
Unit if their sponsors have provisions for such.

Questions about the plan, enrollment, claim should be directed to
Generali Life Assurance (Thailand) Co., Ltd.

Health Declaration (Insured’s Application Form) of each eligible
member will be required and has to be approved by the insurer prior
to the effective date of insurance.

Exclusions of Generali Health Insurance Benefit
No benefit shall be payable under any of the following occurrences:
1.

All dependents have to complete the health declaration form and get approval

The duties of non-
instructional teaching assistants may include assisting faculty in preparing materials for

The qualifications and salary range are as

For a student whose work is not directly related to their own thesis or dissertation, the

Student Assistants shall be students in good standing on the effective dates of their

An Assistant’s contract shall be terminated if, during the period of

A special student who is a master or doctoral student studying at another institution and

Funding

If two or more students compete for a position and they are equally qualified to undertake

The Office of Finance (OFIN) will process the appointment and payment, which must be

Key specific Problem Areas where special effort may be necessary to prevent or
penalize infringement of the five principles.

No member of the AIT community, trustee, faculty, staff, or student, including
outreach program participants, should act deliberately or thoughtlessly to interfere
with the study, work or home environment of other members of the community.

Those with academic or administrative supervisory authority, permanent or
temporary, over faculty, staff or students, should use such authority with respect,

The opportunities, services, facilities and support offered by AIT should be
compatible with the purposes of the Institute, and be open impartially to everyone
in the community according to qualification, performance and achievement.

There
should be no denial or granting of rights or privileges wholly or partly, on irrelevant
grounds such as race, nationality, gender, creed, age, marital status, handicap, or
physical characteristics.

They are thus defined here so that all members of the AIT community
may be aware of what behavior is unacceptable, and take their own steps, as
individuals and group members, to ensure that the Institute's policies, rules and
guidelines are implemented and enforced.

Sexual harassment
1.1 Given the unique diversity of the Institute community, AIT must develop special
awareness and sensitivity to deter or respond to the problem of sexual
harassment.

This is an area where our cultural differences can lead to additional
misunderstanding; for example, certain conduct and communication may be
construed as sexual harassment by some groups that may not be so construed
by others.

supervisor:employee) may not constitute sexual harassment, individuals must
declare such a conflict of interest and remove themselves from the situation.

Informal remedies, with action taken
at the lowest appropriate level, may sometimes be the most effective approach,
and the one preferred by the complainant.

The action taken in each case, which depends
upon the nature and gravity of the conduct reported, may include intervention,
mediation, investigation, and, once the decision is made that informal
procedures are not appropriate, the initiation of formal grievance and
disciplinary processes.

It must be
recognized by all concerned that breaches of confidentiality or privacy in the
informal or formal response to a complaint may divert or even redirect the
remedial action concerned.

1.5 Reprisals against an individual, who in good faith reports or provides information
in an investigation of conduct that may violate Institute policy, will not be
tolerated.

False accusations or false evidence offered in the remedial
process may themselves be considered grounds for disciplinary action.

1.8 Nothing in this policy should be construed as abridging the authority of the
President of the Institute or of presidential designees to an immediate
emergency remedies to ensure the maintenance of order in the event of
Institute disruption, disturbance of the ongoing business and educational
mission of the Institute, or threats to life or property.

Informal procedures
2.1 The complainant of harassment, abuse or discrimination may choose to address
the problem through informal methods because it may be felt that these will
best remedy the questionable conduct concerned.

The main channels for
informal remedial action may be through consultation, direct confrontation or
third-party intervention.

2.2 Consultation refers, in this context, to discussion of problems or potential
problems before recourse to remedial action, informal or formal, but not to
completely informal conversations about problems with seniors, colleagues, line
managers, partners, friends or relatives, although these may, of course, solve
more straightforward problems.

2.3 Preliminary consultation should be available to any member of the Institute
community who wishes to discuss issues related to problem areas.

This way
forward may be chosen because of a desire to keep matters confidential or "off
the record".

The consultation concerned may thus perhaps not identify the
other person(s) involved, sometimes not even the consultor.

2.4 Direct communication refers to the action of an individual who chooses to act
on perceived harassment, abuse or discrimination by addressing the other party
or parties directly, or writing a statement to the individual(s) concerned,
describing the unwelcome behavior and its effect and stating that the behavior
must cease.

2.5 Direct communication may sometimes follow consultation, the complainant
seeking the advice of one of the advisors listed in Section C-2.3 above, on what
to say or write.

The person(s) to whom the direct communication is made may
likewise use consultation and direct communication in their response.

Formal procedures
3.1 A complainant may, with or without previous recourse to informal channels,
decide to seek redress through formal grievance procedures.

If the
counselor concerned, perhaps after consulting others, agrees that formal
grievance procedures may be appropriate, the President or his specifically-
delegated designee, will appoint a grievance committee composed of
chairperson and three to five members.

3.2 Formal grievance procedures may be initiated only if, in the opinion of the
President or his designee, they do not contravene Thai Labor, Civil or Criminal
Law, or other Institute regulations taking precedence over these policies and
procedures.

3.3 Formal complaint statements should be made as soon as possible; the earlier
the report, the easier it is to investigate and take appropriate remedial action.

3.4 The person(s) against whom the complaint has been initiated must be informed
immediately of the complaint by the appointed grievance committee, and be
provided with opportunity for rebuttal.

Such an investigation may only be
initiated on the instructions of the President or his designee, and after the
complainee(s) has (have) received a written general description of the
perceived conduct and the provisions which they are claimed to violate.

Any
evidence to be considered in the investigation should be reported to the
complainee, who should have opportunity to challenge it and/or to present
evidence in defense.

3.6 Matters preliminary to any hearing should normally be decided and notice of
the hearing given as soon as possible, certainly no later than five working days
after the grievance committee has been informed of the case concerned, and
not less than 72 hours prior to the hearing.

Hearings should normally be held
within ten working days of the notice.

3.7 The written notification of the hearing should give: the time and place of the
hearing; the name(s) of the complainant(s); a general description of the
conduct complained of and the policies allegedly infringed; and the name of the
Institute office where any additional information may be obtained.

Findings and recommendations are confidential and shall not be
made public by the Institute or by any participant in a hearing, including the

A tape recording may be kept of the hearing, but will remain
confidential.

3.9 When the complaint is against more than one person, a single hearing may be
held for all complainees, the grievance committee to seek the authorization of
the President for such a consolidation.

If no
satisfactory explanation for the absence is received, or if a complainee leaves
a hearing before its conclusion or adjournment, the hearing proceed without
the individual(s) concerned, and the committee may reach a conclusion based
on the available evidence and make its report accordingly.

3.11 Any relevant evidence may be admitted if it is of the kind on which responsible
persons are accustomed to rely in the conduct of serious affairs, but all evidence
admitted must be justified before it can be used to explain or supplement other
evidence.

No evidence, other than that discussed at the hearing, should be considered by
a grievance committee.

Any hearing participant may be rejected or excluded if he/she refuses to behave
in an orderly manner.

3.13 Following the presentation and examination of evidence at the hearing, a
grievance committee shall agree in private its written report to the President,
which must be submitted within two working days of the termination of the
hearing.

The Report of the grievance committee shall summarize the facts, and
make a determination as to whether the individual(s) concerned did or did not
commit the acts alleged.

The President's decision shall
be final.

This policy shall be
implemented to enforce and monitor the laws of the host country.

The Institute cannot be responsible for the personal lives and decisions of
students.

These people may
be referred to the Director, Office of Student Affairs.
● Treatment may be required in addition to other appropriate disciplinary action.

The AIT Medical Clinic shall serve as the unit for referral for treatment of
substance abuse problems.

The Institute shall be guided by the following:
In case of alcohol abuse:
a.

All incidents of alcohol violations shall be reported to the Director, Office of Student
Affairs in case of students.

Disciplinary Action:
(1) Warning/written reprimand to be placed on student file
(2) Community service
(3) Suspension for 1 semester if behavior leads to injury
(4) Dismissal/Expulsion
The student may appeal thru the Director, Office of Student Affairs to the President.

Students may be subject to arrest and prosecution in cases where the host country’s
laws have been violated.
Expulsion shall be imposed for drug abuse.

In case of gambling:
Gambling is prohibited under the laws of the host country and is not tolerated by the
Institute.

Responsibility centers are required to produce internal systems and
documentation for implementing this policy locally.

Individuals are required to abide by rules and
requirements made under the authority of this policy.

In discharging his/her responsibility to implement this Policy, the President may
be assisted by the Campus Environment and Development Committee.

Current policies and procedures that AIT staff need to follow, and more
information about what the Institute is doing to improve its environmental
performance, may be accessed from the Institute’s Environment web pages.

Any issue or concern involving student
welfare that is related to accommodation should be brought to the attention of
the Director, Office of Student Affairs (OSA), on action required.

For subsequent
semesters, these students may opt to reside off-campus.

Exceptions, on a case-to-case basis, may be given to those have
already exceeded their normal study period.

Families of non-resident students may not reside on campus if the non-resident student is
off-campus.

Master’s or doctoral students with
spouses who are AIT employees must register for staff accommodation.

Students should move with the spouse when staff accommodation becomes
available.

Students, who will study
in another country as part of the Exchange/Dual Degree program, are required
to vacate their rooms while they study abroad.

Exceptions may only be given
to those whose exchange program is less than two months.

Student dormitories may be provided for a
maximum period of one month, in case of availability to:
a.

All guests must be registered with the Accommodation Unit three
days before arrival.

With prior approval of the Accommodation Unit, they may
stay in student dormitory for a maximum quota of one week per semester.

In case of over staying guests, special prior permission must be obtained from
the Director, Office of Facilities and Asset Management (OFAM) and a daily rent
will be charged for the room.

Extra provisions (such as
mattresses, etc.) for the guests may be requested from the Accommodation
Unit, if available and upon payment of a nominal service charge.

The Institute
shall in no way be held responsible for the legal status of the guests.

shall be the responsibility of the guests and not the
Institute.

Students may put their names
in the waiting list and live in staff housing depending on availability.

Students are required to vacate their rooms within
five days (including weekends) after their graduation.

· New students may choose from all the available rooms when their
queue number is called.

New and continuing students wishing to move in
another category of rooms may queue during the period designated by the
Accommodation Unit.

· A student may queue for a maximum of three dorm categories in
order of preference.

· A student may put special requirements in the "specifications"
column of the waiting list form available with the Accommodation Unit.

· A student residing on campus must put his/her name on the
waiting list at the Accommodation Unit in person.

· Only continuing students, who are off-campus on data collection or
on approved leave, may put their names on the waiting list through
email to accommodation@ait.ac.th.

They must provide the approximate
date of their arrival on campus.

Transfer/Relocation According to Waiting List
· An offer for transfer/relocation from one unit to another according
to the waiting list shall start during the period designated by the
Accommodation Unit and is done every Saturday.
· A student shall have five working days to accept the offer.

Failure to
accept the allocated dorm shall result in the removal of the student from
the waiting list.

· A student wishing to move to a new room MUST settle all outstanding
dues related to the previous room, before being assigned a new room.

New and continuing students may apply for student
villages following the policies below:
Shared Unit for Unmarried Students
· Students may apply for a shared unit provided they are able to find
a group of three students in their batch (i.e.

A change in the grouping
after allocation is not permitted unless otherwise approved by the
Director, OFAM.

If married accommodation is not available, new
students may be given the option to reside with their family off-campus
(provided that a prior approval from the Advisor and Director, OFAM has
been granted) or in AIT staff housing units, if available.
Infringement of the above rules may mean sanctions to parties involved.

Sanctions may include withdrawal of names from waiting lists, community
service, formal reprimand, fine, suspension or ban from campus
accommodation.

All maintenance queries must be reported to the Call Center on 02
524 5800 between 0800 and 1700 hours, Monday to Saturday.

Regular cleaning and hygiene of the unit has to be maintained by the
student.

OFAM is authorized to undertake random inspections and may lay
sanctions if the unit is found to be dirty and unhygienic.

Students have to upkeep the cleanliness of the common area and
landscape around the building.
This linen set and pillow may be kept when leaving AIT.

Cooking is not permitted, except in accommodation units rooms
where cooking arrangement is facilities or shared cooking facilities are
provided.

In units where cooking is not permitted, shared cooking facilities
will be explored, when Institute space and resources become available.

Students are not allowed to install heavy appliances, such as air-
conditioners, in accommodation units that are not designed for such facilities,
i.e.

Upon leaving the accommodation units, students are required to
leave their units clean, remove all possessions, and to leave the unit and its
furniture and equipment in the same condition as when the tenancy began.
Students shall not disturb fellow students in the residential area.

Games
and activities shall cease at 2300 hours in all sports fields and courts in and
near campus residential areas.

Noisy group studies and parties are not allowed in student
accommodation.

Alternative rooms designated for such purpose must be
used.

Students who misplace their accommodation unit keys may ask the
Accommodation Unit to open their dorms.

If this service is required after office
hours, i.e.

The sanctions may include withdrawal of names from waiting lists,
community service, formal reprimand, fine, suspension or ban from campus
accommodation.

Graduating students residing beyond the five day limit (including weekends)
after graduation without prior approval from the Director, OFAM may have their
accommodation unit sealed by the Accommodation Unit.

Furniture and appliances should not be removed from the accommodation
unit.

Repair and replacement of furniture and appliances must be requested from
and approved by the Accommodation Unit, which may ask the tenant to pay
additional charges, in situations where damage to furniture and appliances has
resulted from direct negligence by the tenant.

Students who cook in dormitories where cooking is prohibited may face
a minimum fine of Baht 2,000 or the actual costs of the damage caused by
cooking.

accommodation units dormitories not designed for such facilities, will be fined
Baht 2,000 and charged for the services related to the removal of the
appliances.

Students found guilty may face a fine of
Baht 2,000 or the actual costs of the damage caused by smoking or burning.
Pets are not allowed in student accommodation.

Students disturbing the peace of their surroundings shall receive a formal
reprimand as a first warning.

Repeated disturbances may face further
sanctions.
Rooms must be used for residential purposes only.

Sub-letting may result in
eviction from student accommodation.

Students who do not upkeep the standard conditions of the common area
and landscape around the building, particularly with reference to cluttering the
common areas/corridors and planting plants, will be fined Baht 2,000 and
charged for the cleaning and removal services.
Self-funded students are required to pay a one-month advance deposit.

Special and exchange students are required to pay in full the rent for the
whole duration of their stay on campus.

If an ID card is not available, other forms of identification should be
provided.

Any contact
with the police or difficulties off-campus should be reported to the Director, OSA.

As an
international institution, AIT must be neutral on political issues.

While students have
the right to engage in political activities as individuals outside of AIT, they should
not portray themselves or make any claim to represent AIT in such activities.

Proof of identity is required
for most transactions at the bank and may be asked for in the library or at other
campus facilities.
Normally, The AIT Student ID card should be sufficient proof of identity.

Entering Thailand
Students, in receipt of an offer of admission, must obtain a Non-Immigrant VISA
(code "ED") from the Royal Thai Embassy or Consulate-General in their respective
countries.
In addition, the students are not allowed to bring their family member(s)

Upon Arrival
Students are required to submit their passports to the Government Relations Unit
(GRU) within 1 month after the arrival date.

Leaving Thailand
The Institute has a commitment to the Thai Government to ensure that all non-Thai
students must apply for VISA cancellation before leaving Thailand upon completion
of their study programs.

To request for the
VISA cancellation, please contact the GRU office and submit the following
- Original passport
- Clearance form (Retrieved from the SIS system)
- Air ticket
*Students must be presented at the immigration for the VISA cancellation.

A student with the privilege of a return airfare awarded by AIT may lose such benefit
in case of over-staying.

Keeping passport
Non-Thai students may place their passports in the custody of the GRU throughout their
stay in Thailand.

Re-Entry VISAs
Non-Thai students who travel out of Thailand during their period of study should obtain a
Re-entry VISA before EXIT Thailand at:
1.

Pathumthani Immigration Office: Before the departure day, 1 photograph (size 4 x 6
cm., NO T-SHIRT) and Baht 1,000 for single Re-entry VISA or Baht 3,800 for multiple Re-
entry VISA
If no Re-entry VISA is obtained, a NEW application for permission/VISA to stay in Thailand
must be made before the student leaves Thailand.

Students who leave without a Re-entry
VISA MUST follow the steps prescribed above as if they are new students.

Thai VISA Extension Fees
All Non-Thai students are required to pay Baht 1,900 fee for a VISA extension.

Applications for VISA extension of students and their dependents must be submitted to
the GRU, along with the following document.

- Original Passport
- 1 RECENT official photograph (size 4 x 6 cm., NO T-SHIRT)
- Original AIT Transcript
- Original AIT Student Certificate
- Original AIT Tuition Fees / Scholarships
- Original AIT Resident Certificate
The student must contact GRU to proceed with a VISA extension one and half months
before the VISA expiry date.

- Name of Location of the Royal Thai Embassy/ Consulate-General to apply for the
VISA
This information must be given to the GRU as early as possible, and the required fees
should be paid by the student.

The dependent spouse and children of AIT students must apply for an extension of stay at
the GRU office one and a half months before the VISA expiry date.
*All original documents should be in the English Language.

Inquiries about VISAs should be made at the Government Relations Unit (GRU), 1st floor of
the Administration Building, between 7:30 AM - 4:30 PM (Monday– Friday)
Tel: 02-524-5022.

Email: gru@ait.ac.th
Report on Ninety Days of Stay
Students, together with spouses and dependents, if staying in Thailand longer than ninety
days must submit to the Immigration Department reports concerning their place of stay as
soon as possible upon expiration of ninety days.

Students are required to do so every ninety
days of stay.

Students who fail to comply with this immigration provision shall be fined up
to Baht 2,000.

Proper sports attire is required for each sports facility.

Slippers/flip-flops
are not allowed.

Users must follow standard sports rule for each facility or upon mutual
agreement of players depending on need.

First come-first served basis rule must be followed for each court/field and
queuing must be adhered to if there are many players waiting.

For racquet sports, doubles events should be prioritized over single events
if there is a queue.

Sports facilities cannot be used for other sporting events aside from the
intended use of the court/field.
Food and beverages are not allowed in the sports facility.

Food may be
consumed on the sitting area only.

Last players of the evening must turn off the lights and other electrical
equipment of the court.

Student organizations who wish to book the courts/fields must seek
permission from the Student Union and inform the community before its
use.

The users of the pool should only be AIT community members which
includes AIT staff, students and their families and visitors at AITCC.
Outsiders are not allowed.

All users have to register before using the swimming pool and the
swimming pool staff may also request for the AIT ID verification.
Proper swim wear is required.
Food and beverages are not allowed in the pool.
Bringing liquor or any intoxicating drinks is prohibited.
Individuals intoxicated with liquor are not permitted to use the pool.
Smoking is not permitted at the pool.

Children must be accompanied and supervised by parents or guardians at
all times.

Inexperienced swimmer must be accompanied by an
experienced swimmer.

Running and playing around the edge of the pool are not allowed as these
may result in injury due to falling in the water or falling in the concrete.

For training sessions, prior permission has to be taken from OFAM and
trainer might need to pay for using the facilities.

Students or staff should book for use of the carts through the SU
Secretary, with a deposit of Baht 500 and can be collected after the usage of the
cart.

Anyone wishing to use the
courier service should contact the AIT Campus Mail Services Office and bring
the intended shipment during office hours.

Therefore, all AIT degree
program students are required to fulfill the English requirements before they
can graduate.

If you score 5.0, you are required to attend and successfully
complete EL 15 and EL 19 before you can graduate.

The LC also offers a range of elective academic writing courses each semester
and during term breaks, all covered under the 2,000 Baht Language Support
Fee students are required to pay in the first semester.

Before Graduation: All students must have their advisor approve and sign the
title page of their research report, thesis or dissertation, and then submit the
signed title page to the LC for title-checking.

Finally, they must submit the title
page signed by their advisor and by LC to the AIT Registry.

When using Internet, students have to adhere to the Institute’s Policies & Procedures
on the Use of Computing and Network Services at AIT whose details can be viewed
from http://intranet.ait.ac.th/administration/policies-and-procedures/AA/aa-9-4-1-
use-of-computing-and-network-services-at-agt-7jun2021.pdf
Internet access from academic/administrative buildings is restricted for academic
purposes only.

Nevertheless, adherence to the host country’s (Thai) laws still must be
strictly observed.

Students may also avail of Microsoft Office 365 licenses through Microsoft’s Student
Advantage Program where every student is entitled to install Microsoft Office 365 on
up to 5 devices, including PC, Mac, Tablets and Smart Phones.

Details of IT services mentioned above as well other information are available on
Helpdesk website https://helpdesk.ait.ac.th/
Students encountering difficulties in using Internet and other campus wide IT services
above shall contact Helpdesk preferably by sending email to helpdesk@ait.ac.th.

Student’s Insurance:

In emergency cases or if any medical emergency
assistance is required, the nurse on duty can be contacted at 02-524-5555.

The AIT campus includes a diverse mixture of settings,
from offices, classrooms and laboratories to houses and dormitories, and each
setting may present potential hazards.

You should not work alone
in the laboratory, especially at night, without written permission from
the Laboratory Supervisor.

If you
have to use an outside technician, ask for a company ID card.

● Your guests should know your personal details (name, house/room
number, telephone, School/Program, etc.), for security reasons.

♦ In order to avoid any untoward incident, firecrackers can be set-off
only at the basketball court near SV3.

♦ Organizers shall be responsible to clear all the trash and waste from
the fire crackers after the event.

♦ Organizers of the event shall be responsible for any untoward
incident that may occur in relation to this activity.
● Any safety and security concerns should be reported to Security Officer.

For further assistance for CCTV footage or assistance to police station,
permission should be sought from Call Center, OFAM.

The offender
is required to present an AIT or valid ID card to the security on duty.

SHOPPING AND OTHER SERVICES
On Campus
There are several shops on campus where you can get basic supplies for daily
living, but quality and choice may be limited and prices higher than in local
markets in commercial areas.
